Prolonged coral bleaching lasting more than 7 days typically results in coral mortality rates exceeding 50 percent across affected reef areas. The 2016 global bleaching event impacted approximately 30 percent of the world's tropical coral reefs through decreased biomass and structural degradation.

Pitfalls to keep in mind:
- Instrumental temperature records before the mid-twentieth century contain spatial coverage gaps that introduce uncertainty into historical baselines.
